title: Roasted, Aerated Venezuelan Onions Casserole
description: A savory, aerated, hangover-cure Venezuelan dish featuring onions, marinated in a mixture of garlic, cilantro, and lime juice, then baked in a tagliere.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together lime juice, garlic, cilantro, salt, black pepper, and crushed red pepper. Add the sliced onions and toss to coat.
Let the onions marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.
Meanwhile, grease a 9x13-inch tagliere with butter. Sprinkle the flour evenly over the bottom of the tagliere.
Arrange the marinated onions in the tagliere, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, shredded mozzarella cheese, and grated cheddar cheese.
Pour the egg mixture over the onions in the tagliere.
Bake the casserole for 35-40 minutes, or until the eggs are set and the top is golden brown.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
